brand.name: SolForge Crypto
brand.tagline: A library for reading crypto markets
snapshot.title: Current market snapshot
snapshot.note: Data comes from the public CoinGecko and Alternative.me APIs. Values may be delayed or vary with provider methodology and are not investment advice.
path.title: Continue with these resources
footer.disclaimer: Educational material that explains public data. It does not provide investment recommendations or promise returns.
dynamic.partial: Some live data could not be loaded. The explanatory resources below remain available.
dynamic.unavailable: The data provider did not respond, so the current value is unavailable.
dynamic.noAssets: The asset list could not be loaded.
pages.home.meta.title: Crypto Market Briefing and Learning Guides | SolForge Crypto
pages.home.meta.description: Review total crypto market cap, volume, BTC dominance and the Fear & Greed Index, then learn what each signal means and where it can mislead.
pages.home.hero.title: Go beyond displaying numbers and learn how to read them.
pages.home.hero.body: Follow a current market snapshot with Bitcoin and Ethereum primers and practical guides to volatility, custody and smart-contract risk. The focus is on verification order and data limits rather than trading signals.
pages.home.hero.noteBody: We do not provide executable prices or recommend buying or selling an asset. Every number is paired with its source and interpretive limits.
pages.home.reading.title: Four checks to make before interpreting today's numbers
pages.home.reading.intro: Compare signals with different purposes at the same point in time instead of inferring direction from one metric.
pages.home.sections.marketCap.title: Market cap describes the size of the market
pages.home.sections.marketCap.body: A higher total market cap does not mean every asset rose together. Pair the 24-hour change with BTC and ETH shares to see whether value is broadening across the market or concentrating in large assets.
pages.home.sections.dominance.title: Dominance measures concentration
pages.home.sections.dominance.body: Rising BTC dominance means Bitcoin represents a larger relative share, not that its price must be rising. Dominance can increase when the whole market falls and altcoins fall faster.
pages.home.sections.volume.title: Volume is evidence of participation intensity
pages.home.sections.volume.body: Check whether price and volume expanded together. Providers and exchanges aggregate volume differently, and reported totals may include derivatives or questionable activity, so trends on one consistent basis are more useful than isolated totals.
pages.home.sections.sentiment.title: Sentiment is supporting context, not a standalone signal
pages.home.sections.sentiment.body: The Fear & Greed Index compresses market mood into one number. Extremes do not guarantee a reversal, so compare price, volume and multiple timeframes and ask whether the condition has persisted.
pages.marketContext.meta.title: How to Read Crypto Market Indicators | SolForge Crypto
pages.marketContext.meta.description: Learn how to compare market cap, volume, BTC dominance and the Fear & Greed Index on a consistent basis and understand their limitations.
pages.marketContext.hero.title: How to connect crypto market indicators
pages.marketContext.hero.body: Market cap, volume, dominance and sentiment answer different questions. Identify what each number measures, then compare values using the same time window and currency basis.
pages.marketContext.hero.noteBody: Review total market change, volume, BTC share, major-asset changes and sentiment in that order to reduce duplicate interpretations.
pages.marketContext.reading.title: The question each indicator answers
pages.marketContext.reading.intro: Measurement targets and aggregation methods differ, so do not force every movement into one explanation.
pages.marketContext.sections.marketCap.t: Total market cap: did the market's size change?
pages.marketContext.sections.marketCap.b: This is the sum of each asset's circulating supply multiplied by its current price. Supply changes and newly tracked assets also affect the total, so it is not identical to net cash entering the market.
pages.marketContext.sections.volume.body: Check whether a short-term price move came with higher volume. Do not mix totals from different providers; compare daily or weekly changes using one aggregation method.
pages.marketContext.sections.dominance.b: This is BTC or ETH market cap as a share of the total market. Both numerator and denominator move, so a ratio change cannot determine the direction of one asset's price.
pages.marketContext.sections.sentiment.t: Fear & Greed: what is the prevailing mood?
pages.marketContext.sections.sentiment.b: This provider-specific reference combines inputs such as volatility, volume and attention. Extreme fear or greed can persist, and the calculation method may change.
pages.marketContext.sections.timeframe.t: Timeframe: the same move can tell different stories
pages.marketContext.sections.timeframe.b: A 24-hour gain may be a rebound inside a weekly decline. Separate daily, weekly and monthly views and confirm that comparison windows and time zones match.
pages.marketContext.sections.checklist.b: Verify source, update time, base currency, spot or derivatives coverage, supply changes and exchange price differences. Recheck unexplained spikes against the original provider and official announcements.
pages.bitcoin.meta.title: Bitcoin Structure and Market Checks | SolForge Crypto
pages.bitcoin.meta.description: A primer on Bitcoin issuance, network security, market indicators and the main risks to verify before relying on a chart.
pages.bitcoin.hero.title: Understand Bitcoin as a network, not only a price ticker
pages.bitcoin.hero.body: Bitcoin is a public network that shares transaction records without a central operator. Learn what its supply cap and proof-of-work consensus mean and which market details require verification.
pages.bitcoin.hero.noteBody: Protocol operation, exchange pricing and personal custody are separate layers. Trust in one layer does not automatically make another layer safe.
pages.bitcoin.reading.title: Separate structure, market and risk
pages.bitcoin.reading.intro: Start with verifiable network rules and the scope of market data rather than a price forecast.
pages.bitcoin.sections.purpose.title: Purpose and function
pages.bitcoin.sections.purpose.body: Bitcoin is designed to validate records of value transfer without an intermediary. Anyone can inspect network rules and transactions, but throughput and settlement timing have structural constraints.
pages.bitcoin.sections.issuance.title: Supply cap and halvings
pages.bitcoin.sections.issuance.body: The protocol defines a maximum supply and declining block rewards. A halving reduces new issuance but does not guarantee a price outcome because demand, liquidity and the macro environment move independently.
pages.bitcoin.sections.network.title: Proof of work and network security
pages.bitcoin.sections.network.body: Miners spend computing resources to compete for block production while nodes verify rules. Hash rate is one security clue, but mining concentration, the fee market and software diversity also matter.
pages.bitcoin.sections.signals.title: What to verify in market data
pages.bitcoin.sections.signals.body: Check spot price and volume, BTC's share of the total market and the source of exchange flow data. On-chain metrics are also limited because one address does not necessarily equal one user.
pages.bitcoin.sections.risks.title: Main risks
pages.bitcoin.sections.risks.body: High volatility, exchange failure, lost keys, regulatory change, derivatives liquidation and liquidity gaps can cause loss. A functioning network does not mean every custody service is safe.
pages.bitcoin.sections.checklist.title: Checklist for any Bitcoin resource
pages.bitcoin.sections.checklist.body: Record the pricing exchange, spot or futures basis, supply definition and update time. Do not treat one chart or a calendar event as an independent reason to trade.
pages.ethereum.meta.title: Ethereum Structure and Market Checks | SolForge Crypto
pages.ethereum.meta.description: Learn how Ethereum smart contracts, fees, proof of stake and ecosystem metrics work, along with their technical and market risks.
pages.ethereum.hero.title: Understand Ethereum as both an asset and an execution platform
pages.ethereum.hero.body: Ethereum is a public network for running smart contracts, and ETH pays fees and helps secure the network. Separate ecosystem activity from the market price of ETH.
pages.ethereum.hero.noteBody: Ethereum mainnet, layer 2 networks, individual apps, bridges and tokens have different risk owners. Identify the layer behind every metric.
pages.ethereum.reading.title: A framework for interpreting network activity
pages.ethereum.reading.intro: Ask what ecosystem figures such as fees and deposited assets include, exclude and count more than once.
pages.ethereum.sections.purpose.title: A smart-contract platform
pages.ethereum.sections.purpose.body: Programs can be deployed to a public network and execute under predefined conditions. Results are verifiable, but this does not remove contract bugs or privileged operator risks.
pages.ethereum.sections.fees.title: Gas fees and network demand
pages.ethereum.sections.fees.body: Gas pays for computation and storage. Higher fees can indicate more activity, but congestion, one application event or automated trading can also be the cause.
pages.ethereum.sections.consensus.title: Proof of stake and validators
pages.ethereum.sections.consensus.body: Validators deposit ETH and participate in proposing and validating blocks. Staking yield is not risk-free; it includes price, slashing, operational and custody risk.
pages.ethereum.sections.ecosystem.title: Layer 2 networks and applications
pages.ethereum.sections.ecosystem.body: Layer 2 systems execute transactions separately and settle results to mainnet. When comparing TVL or transaction counts, check duplicate counting, bridged assets and incentive effects.
pages.ethereum.sections.signals.title: What to verify in market data
pages.ethereum.sections.signals.body: Treat ETH dominance, spot volume, network fees, staking share and supply changes as different indicators. Higher ecosystem use does not directly guarantee a short-term price increase.
pages.ethereum.sections.risks.title: Main risks
pages.ethereum.sections.risks.body: Smart-contract flaws, bridge exploits, centralized operator keys, validator concentration, regulatory change and volatility all matter. Recheck approvals and official addresses before using an app.
pages.risk.meta.title: Crypto Risks to Understand Before Investing | SolForge Crypto
pages.risk.meta.description: A practical guide to volatility, leverage, custody, smart-contract, stablecoin and liquidity risk with points to verify.
pages.risk.hero.title: Understand how loss can happen before focusing on returns
pages.risk.hero.body: Even without a price decline, exchange outages, lost keys, contract failures or depegging can damage value or access. Separate price, trading, custody and protocol risk.
pages.risk.hero.noteBody: Avoid yield you cannot explain, deposits with unclear exit terms and excessive leverage. Never share a recovery phrase or authentication code.
pages.risk.reading.title: Risk types and verification points
pages.risk.reading.intro: Several risks can occur together, so review each stage of holding, trading and depositing assets separately.
pages.risk.sections.volatility.title: Price volatility
pages.risk.sections.volatility.body: A 24-hour market can move sharply in a short period. Separate living expenses from risk capital and never assume you can sell at a displayed price during stress.
pages.risk.sections.leverage.title: Leverage and forced liquidation
pages.risk.sections.leverage.body: Borrowed exposure magnifies small price moves. Verify maintenance margin, liquidation price, funding costs and likely execution price during a rapid move for each venue.
pages.risk.sections.custody.title: Exchange and self-custody
pages.risk.sections.custody.body: Exchange custody carries freeze and operator-failure risk; self-custody carries loss and phishing risk. Match methods to asset size and usage and test recovery with a small amount.
pages.risk.sections.contracts.title: Smart contracts and bridges
pages.risk.sections.contracts.body: An audit does not guarantee the absence of vulnerabilities. Check operator privileges, upgradeability, withdrawal terms, official contract addresses and token approval scope.
pages.risk.sections.stablecoin.title: Stablecoin depegging
pages.risk.sections.stablecoin.body: Price stability may rely on cash-like reserves, crypto collateral or algorithms. Review reserve composition, redemption counterparties, attestations and market liquidity.
pages.risk.sections.liquidity.title: Liquidity, fraud and outages
pages.risk.sections.liquidity.body: Displayed and executable prices may differ. Treat thin markets, withdrawal suspensions, cloned websites and guaranteed-return claims as warning signs, and verify addresses and notices through multiple channels.
pages.methodology.meta.title: Data Sources and Editorial Policy | SolForge Crypto
pages.methodology.meta.description: How SolForge Crypto handles public data sources, refresh timing, currencies, missing values and editorial independence.
pages.methodology.hero.title: We publish the source of each number and its limits
pages.methodology.hero.body: The browser loads the market snapshot from public APIs, while explanatory resources are edited around metric definitions and general risk structures. The static guides remain readable during provider outages.
pages.methodology.hero.noteBody: Before trading or making a decision, check your exchange's live order book, official project documentation and notices from the relevant regulator.
pages.methodology.reading.title: How we handle data and explanations
pages.methodology.reading.intro: Reproducible sources, update times, visible failure states and disclosure of incentives are the baseline.
pages.methodology.sections.sources.title: Public data sources
pages.methodology.sections.sources.body: Total market cap, volume, dominance and major-asset prices use CoinGecko; sentiment uses Alternative.me. Results can change when a provider changes classifications or calculations.
pages.methodology.sections.refresh.title: Refresh and caching
pages.methodology.sections.refresh.body: The page requests a recent response when opened and may use a short browser cache. The displayed check time can be when the response arrived, not when every underlying value was calculated.
pages.methodology.sections.currency.body: Market cap and volume use abbreviated USD values by default. Converted values reflect provider exchange rates and aggregation timing and can differ from an executable KRW amount.
pages.methodology.sections.missing.title: Missing and delayed data
pages.methodology.sections.missing.body: We show an unavailable state instead of filling failed requests or missing fields with estimates. Static explanations and source notes remain available when live cards are empty.
pages.methodology.sections.editorial.bod: We do not promise returns or present buy and sell timing. Definitions, verification steps, counterexamples and limits are presented in the same structure in Korean and English.
pages.methodology.sections.disclaimer.bo: Confirmed errors are corrected after rechecking the source and methodology. This is educational information, not investment, legal or tax advice tailored to an individual.
pages.about.meta.title: About | SolForge Crypto
pages.about.meta.description: The mission, coverage, bilingual operation and editorial independence of SolForge Crypto.
pages.about.hero.title: An independent library for understanding crypto market numbers
pages.about.hero.body: SolForge Crypto prioritizes explanations that help readers understand public market material, concepts and risks over calculators that require users to enter their own values.
pages.about.hero.noteBody: We explain what each indicator measures, the order for checking it, exceptions and limits rather than reproducing short fragments of information.
pages.about.reading.title: What the site provides
pages.about.reading.intro: Market briefings, primers and risk resources are linked so a first-time visitor can follow a clear reading path.
pages.about.sections.mission.title: Mission
pages.about.sections.mission.body: We provide verification criteria and sources so readers can judge the meaning and limits of data instead of seeing price figures alone.
pages.about.sections.scope.title: Coverage
pages.about.sections.scope.body: We prioritize total-market indicators, Bitcoin and Ethereum fundamentals, custody, trading and protocol risk, and transparent data methodology.
pages.about.sections.bilingual.title: Korean and English
pages.about.sections.bilingual.body: Every core page follows the same Korean and English structure, and the language switch preserves the current page path.
pages.about.sections.independence.title: Editorial independence
pages.about.sections.independence.body: No exchange or token sponsors rankings on this site. Google AdSense ads are displayed separately from editorial material.
pages.privacy.meta.title: Privacy | SolForge Crypto
pages.privacy.meta.description: How SolForge Crypto handles user input, external APIs, hosting logs and Google advertising technologies.
pages.privacy.hero.title: Keep collection limited and make external connections clear
pages.privacy.hero.body: The site currently requires no account or personal investment information. It uses external public APIs and Cloudflare hosting to present the market snapshot.
pages.privacy.hero.noteBody: Never enter a recovery phrase, private key, exchange password or authentication code on this site. SolForge does not request them.
pages.privacy.reading.title: Current processing scope
pages.privacy.reading.intro: This policy will be updated to match actual behavior if features or advertising change.
pages.privacy.sections.inputs.title: User input
pages.privacy.sections.inputs.body: The independent crypto site does not currently collect or store accounts, portfolios, contact details or payment information on a server.
pages.privacy.sections.apis.title: External public APIs
pages.privacy.sections.apis.body: The browser may request CoinGecko and Alternative.me directly for the snapshot, and those providers may process connection information under their own policies.
pages.privacy.sections.hosting.title: Hosting and access logs
pages.privacy.sections.hosting.body: Cloudflare Pages delivers the content and may process basic access logs for security, reliability and statistics under Cloudflare's policies.
pages.privacy.sections.advertising.title: Advertising and cookies
pages.privacy.sections.advertising.body: This site uses the Google AdSense publisher code. Google and other advertising providers may use cookies or similar technologies to serve and measure ads. Ads are managed so they do not cover the main explanations or market data.
pages.privacy.sections.contact.title: Contact and policy changes
pages.privacy.sections.contact.body: Privacy questions can be submitted through the main SolForge contact page. Changes in processing will be reflected here with the applicable timing.
